diagrams.title: Project Estimation Process Flow
diagrams.description: A flowchart showing the sequential steps in project estimation, from gathering requirements to final effort and schedule estimates. This helps visualize how estimation builds upon previous activities and feeds into scheduling.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the iterative and sequential nature of the estimation process in software projects

diagrams.title: Project Scheduling Timeline with Milestones
diagrams.description: A horizontal timeline diagram displaying project phases (Planning, Design, Development, Testing, Deployment) with tasks, milestones, and the critical path highlighted. Shows how tasks overlap and dependencies affect the schedule.
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes how software project schedules are constructed with phases, tasks, and milestones

diagrams.title: Estimation Techniques Comparison
diagrams.description: A comparative diagram showing different estimation techniques (Analogous, Parametric, Bottom-Up, Wideband Delphi) arranged by accuracy vs effort required. Shows when each technique is most appropriate in the project lifecycle.
diagrams.purpose: Helps project managers select appropriate estimation techniques based on project phase and available information
